query: CDC NoroSTAT 2025-2026 season cumulative norovirus outbreaks latest data update May June 2026
answer:
## CDC NoroSTAT 2025-2026 Season: Cumulative Norovirus Outbreak Data (Latest Update: May 20, 2026)

---

### Key Finding

**During August 1, 2025 – May 7, 2026, there were 1,194 norovirus outbreaks reported by the 14 NoroSTAT-participating states**, according to data last updated on **May 20, 2026**. This is roughly **53% fewer** than the 2,534 outbreaks reported during the same period in the 2024-2025 season. However, CDC notes the 2025-2026 total falls **within the middle 50% (interquartile range)** of outbreaks reported during the same period across the 2012-2025 seasonal years — indicating the prior season was anomalously high, not that the current season is anomalously low.

---

### Participating States (14 total)

Colorado, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Nebraska, New Hampshire, New Mexico, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and Wisconsin.

---

### Season Trajectory: Weekly Outbreak Counts

The season showed a characteristic winter peak:

| Period | Trend | Notable Data Points |
|--------|-------|-------------------|
| **Aug–Sep 2025** | Low baseline activity | 4–13 outbreaks/week; within or below IQR |
| **Oct 2025** | Rising | Week of Oct 31: **36 outbreaks** — exceeded the prior historical maximum (33) for that week |
| **Nov–Dec 2025** | Seasonal ramp-up | 15–55 outbreaks/week; generally within IQR |
| **Jan 2026** | Acceleration | 39–68/week; week of Jan 30 (68) exceeded 75th percentile (61) |
| **Feb 2026 (peak)** | **Season peak** | Week of Feb 6: **78 outbreaks** — above 75th percentile (63), near historical max (117) |
| **Mar 2026** | Declining | 33–41/week; dropping back toward 25th percentile |
| **Apr 2026** | Low/waning | 22–28/week; within or below IQR |
| **May 1–7, 2026** | Baseline | **19 outbreaks** in most recent reported week |

---

### Comparison to Previous Season

| Metric | 2025-2026 (Aug 1–May 7) | 2024-2025 (same period) |
|--------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| Cumulative outbreaks | **1,194** | **2,534** |
| Difference | **–53%** (1,340 fewer) | — |
| vs. 2012–2025 historical range | Within IQR (normal) | Near or above historical maximum |

---

### Major Genotype Shift (CaliciNet Data)

A significant strain shift occurred between seasons:

| Genotype | 2024-2025 (n=411) | 2025-2026 (n=102, through Apr 30) |
|----------|-------------------|-----------------------------------|
| **GII.17[P17]** | **74.5%** (306 outbreaks) | **19.6%** (20 outbreaks) |
| **GII.4 Sydney[P16]** | **10.5%** (43 outbreaks) | **52.0%** (53 outbreaks) |
| **GII.7[P7]** | — | **6.9%** (7 outbreaks) |

**GII.4 Sydney[P16] has regained dominance** in 2025-2026 after GII.17[P17] was the dominant strain in the prior season. This genotype shift likely contributes to the different outbreak dynamics between seasons.

---

### Methodology Notes

- NoroSTAT has been operational since **August 2012**.
- States report preliminary epidemiologic data to CDC through **NORS** within 7 business days.
- Laboratory confirmation reported through **CaliciNet** within 7 business days.
- Data is updated **monthly**.
- Content source: CDC's **National Center for Immunization and Respiratory Diseases (NCIRD), Division of Viral Diseases**.

---

### Background: Annual U.S. Norovirus Burden (CDC Standing Estimates)

- **19–21 million** illnesses annually
- **~109,000** hospitalizations
- **~900** deaths (mostly adults 65+)
- **~2,500** outbreaks reported per year on average
- **58%** of U.S. foodborne illness attributed to norovirus
- **~$2 billion** annual economic cost

---

key_facts: During August 1, 2025 – May 7, 2026, there were 1,194 norovirus outbreaks reported by NoroSTAT-participating states (data last updated May 20, 2026).
The 2025-2026 cumulative total (1,194) is 53% lower than the same period in 2024-2025 (2,534 outbreaks), but falls within the historical interquartile range (middle 50%) of 2012-2025 seasonal years.
14 state health departments participate in NoroSTAT: CO, MA, MI, MN, NE, NH, NM, NC, OH, OR, SC, TN, VA, WI.
The season peaked in the week of February 6, 2026 with 78 outbreaks, exceeding the 75th percentile (63) for that week.
The week of October 31, 2025 saw 36 outbreaks, setting a new historical maximum for that calendar week (previous max was 33).
A major genotype shift occurred: GII.4 Sydney[P16] regained dominance in 2025-2026 at 52.0% of confirmed outbreaks (n=102), displacing GII.17[P17] which had dominated 2024-2025 at 74.5% (n=411).
GII.17[P17] dropped from 74.5% of outbreaks in 2024-2025 to 19.6% in 2025-2026.
The most recent reported week (May 1-7, 2026) had 19 outbreaks, consistent with typical seasonal decline.
By March-April 2026, weekly outbreak counts had fallen back to or below the 25th percentile of the historical range.
CDC NoroSTAT data is updated monthly; the latest update was May 20, 2026.
